preamble: The shift from glottal stop [ʔ] to glottal fricative [h] is glottal spirantization, a form of lenition where the laryngeal closure weakens. Both sounds are laryngeal; the shift involves losing the complete closure, producing continuous frication instead.

examples.note: Historical development where glottal stops weaken to fricatives or zero.
Occasional weakening of the hamza in rapid speech or specific coda positions.

sources: Holes, C. (2004). Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties. Georgetown University Press.
